skill.name: jwt-auth
skill.description: Guides implementation of JWT authentication for FastAPI and Python projects, including token creation, verification dependencies, current-user extraction, and role checks.
skill.summary: Implement JWT authentication patterns for FastAPI and Python backends.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: The static weak-crypto and external-command detections are false positives from regex usage and Markdown code formatting. The skill is still security-sensitive because it guides JWT authentication and environment-based secret configuration, so publication should include a warning to validate generated auth code before production use.

medium_findings.title: Security-Sensitive JWT Implementation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill provides guidance for JWT generation, verification, role checks, and secret configuration. This is legitimate functionality, but generated authentication code can create production risk if expiration, claim contents, revocation, or secret handling are implemented incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes JWT security controls and environment variables for token secrets. This is not malicious, but the domain is security-critical and needs human validation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches
low_findings.description: The detections in scripts/verify.py are regular expressions for validating skill frontmatter, not cryptographic operations. The SKILL.md references HS256 for JWTs; this is a normal HMAC JWT algorithm when paired with a strong secret, not evidence of malicious cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged Python lines call re.search, re.match, and string checks only. The JWT algorithm references are documentation examples rather than hidden or executable cryptographic misuse.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code References Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external-command detections in SKILL.md are inline Markdown identifiers and Python examples. They do not execute shell commands, spawn processes, or instruct the assistant to run untrusted commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious locations are documentation tables, fenced Python examples, and inline code spans. No backtick shell execution syntax appears in executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Local File Validation Script
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detection is a local verifier checking whether SKILL.md and scripts/verify.py exist. It reads local skill metadata and does not enumerate sensitive directories or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script derives a skill path, checks expected files, and exits with validation messages. There is no network use, credential access, or broad system discovery.

content.value_statement: JWT authentication is easy to implement incorrectly in Python backends. This skill gives structured FastAPI patterns for tokens, dependencies, current-user extraction, and role checks.
actual_capabilities: Creates FastAPI patterns for JWT access token generation.
Shows token verification dependencies for protected routes.
Explains current-user extraction from JWT claims.
Documents role-based access control using allowed roles.
Lists JWT configuration variables for secrets, algorithm, and expiration.
Includes a local verifier for required skill metadata.
limitations: Does not implement OAuth2 provider setup or OpenID Connect flows.
Does not provide a complete refresh-token architecture.
Does not replace a production security review of generated auth code.
Targets FastAPI and Python backends, not other frameworks.

use_cases.title: Add Protected FastAPI Routes
use_cases.description: Create token verification dependencies and attach them to routes that require authenticated users.

use_cases.title: Standardize JWT Claims
use_cases.description: Define subject, expiration, role, and custom claim handling for a Python API.

use_cases.title: Review Auth Implementation Steps
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to confirm token expiration, secret handling, claim safety, and logout behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic JWT Auth
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new FastAPI service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add basic JWT authentication to my FastAPI project. Include token creation, verification, and a protected route.

prompt_templates.title: Add Current User Dependency
prompt_templates.scenario: Existing API needs user extraction from bearer tokens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a current-user dependency that validates a bearer token and returns a user object from JWT claims.

prompt_templates.title: Add Role Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add role-based access control for admin and editor routes in my FastAPI backend.

prompt_templates.title: Audit JWT Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my FastAPI JWT implementation for expiration, secret configuration, sensitive claims, and logout invalidation.

output_examples.input: Request to add JWT login protection to a FastAPI API.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering token creation, verification dependency wiring, protected route usage, and required environment settings.

output_examples.input: Request to add role checks for admin-only endpoints.
output_examples.output: A role-based access pattern that validates the current user and rejects requests without required roles.

output_examples.input: Request to review JWT settings before launch.
output_examples.output: A checklist-style review of token lifetime, secret strength, claim contents, algorithm configuration, and revocation behavior.

best_practices: Use a long random JWT secret stored outside source control.
Keep access tokens short lived and add refresh or revocation where needed.
Store only minimal non-sensitive claims in each token.
anti_patterns: Do not place passwords, personal data, or API keys inside JWT claims.
Do not disable expiration checks or accept tokens without signatures.
Do not use generated auth code in production without tests and review.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets FastAPI and Python backend projects that use JWT bearer authentication.

faq.question: Does it support OAuth2 providers?
faq.answer: No. The skill excludes OAuth2 provider setup and OpenID Connect integration.

faq.question: Can it add role-based access control?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes role checks based on role claims stored in the JWT payload.

faq.question: Does it manage secrets automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents required environment variables, but the user must manage secret storage securely.

faq.question: Is HS256 always safe?
faq.answer: HS256 can be safe with a strong secret and correct verification. Production systems should validate algorithm handling carefully.

faq.question: Should generated auth code be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. Authentication code should be tested and reviewed before production use.
